_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — SP-760005: xp_cmdshell enabled in MSSQL (T1059 — OS command execution via DB)
scenario: Attacker or malware enables xp_cmdshell in MSSQL Server to execute OS commands directly from SQL queries

event.statement: EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; RECONFIGURE; EXEC sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; RECONFIGURE;

why_tp: xp_cmdshell is disabled by default in all modern MSSQL installations — any enablement is suspicious
Executed via the 'sa' account — SQL Server system administrator, highest privilege
Client IP 10.10.100.55 is a workstation, not a known DBA management host
Confidence 95: xp_cmdshell activation rarely has legitimate use cases in production
analyst_action: IMMEDIATE: Disable xp_cmdshell: EXEC sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 0; RECONFIGURE;
Check what commands were executed via xp_cmdshell before detection
Investigate how the 'sa' credentials were obtained
Check for new Windows accounts, scheduled tasks, or files created by MSSQL service account
Review MSSQL audit log for all statements from client 10.10.100.55
